title: artemisia ludoviciana subsp. sulcata
intro: Artemisia ludoviciana subsp. sulcata, also known as French tarragon or wormwood, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is a member of the sunflower family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to repel insects and parasites. The health benefits of Artemisia ludoviciana subsp. sulcata include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
